events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario involves transformers (dry-type & pad-mount) at an industrial site, work in-progress, with observed field condition: inadequate ventilation clearance. References: NEC Art. 450 (transformers) and 250.30 (grounding separately derived systems).

Inadequate ventilation clearance is a non-compliance issue - NEC 450 requires proper clearances for ventilation around transformers. This is a safety hazard that must be corrected before work continues.

Decision: fail (non-compliant)
Risk: high (overheating/fire hazard)
Work stage: in-progress
Component conditions: non-compliant (the installation doesn't meet clearance requirements)
Percent complete: maybe 60% (in-progress, transformers installed but clearance inadequate)

Findings: inadequate ventilation clearance around transformers
Actions: stop work, reconfigure installation to meet manufacturer and NEC clearance requirements, verify clearances before energizing
Rationale: NEC Art. 450 requires adequate ventilation clearances for transformers; inadequate clearance risks overheating and fire
References: NEC Art. 450, NEC 250.30

  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop transformer installation/energization work in the affected area",
    "Verify required ventilation clearances against manufacturer nameplate data and NEC Art. 450",
    "Reconfigure transformer placement, relocate obstructions, or modify enclosure/room ventilation to achieve compliant clearances",
    "Confirm grounding and bonding of separately derived systems per NEC 250.30 before any energization",
    "Re-inspect and document clearances before resuming work"
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC Art. 450 mandates adequate ventilation and working clearances for transformers. Inadequate clearance is a code violation and a thermal/fire safety hazard. Work cannot continue until clearances are corrected and verified, and grounding per 250.30 is confirmed for separately derived systems.",
